title: Ledger uses two-tier money rounding: half-up at input, half-even for arithmetic
content: ai-bookkeeper ledger now has two rounding helpers by design: (1) amount.ts's private roundToCents uses Math.round = half-UP, applied only at the single-value input boundary where tie-bias can't accumulate; (2) round.ts roundMoney(value, currency) uses banker's half-to-EVEN rounding to currency minor units, for aggregation/allocation paths where half-up's upward bias would skew totals (the sub-cent drift risk ADR 0001 flagged). Canonical money representation stays a signed 2-decimal number — both helpers preserve it, so no consumer churn. Integer-cents migration remains deferred to ADR 0001's trigger (persistence). Convention: corrupt money values (NaN/Infinity) FAIL LOUD with RangeError, never silently propagate. Open follow-up (Task 4, 2pts): converge roundToCents onto roundMoney once a real aggregation consumer exists. Currency minor units: most=2, JPY/KRW/CLP/ISK/VND=0, BHD/KWD/OMR/TND=3.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 18:45 UTC
Architect handoffs may arrive as uncommitted on-disk code — Dev Clone's job is verify+commit, not re-implement
content: In team mode for ai-bookkeeper, the Architect persona writes implementation files to the working tree but leaves them UNCOMMITTED (git status: untracked lib files + modified barrel). Dev Clone's value-add when picking up such a handoff is: (1) verify the code is actually on disk and matches the handoff, (2) run `npm test` (tsx --test) to confirm green, (3) confirm typecheck clean for the new module (ignore the documented pre-existing e2e/benchmarks-compare TS2783 error), (4) commit ONLY the scoped files. Do NOT re-write code that's already correct, and do NOT pull unrelated untracked files into the scoped commit. Recurring trap: a barrel index.ts may re-export sibling modules (amount.ts, ingest.ts) that are themselves untracked from a prior work order — committing the barrel alone makes a fresh checkout non-compiling. Flag this cross-work-order integration gap via manual_review rather than silently widening scope.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 18:51 UTC
ai-bookkeeper security baseline: clean source, only transitive postcss CVE
content: Security scan of ai-bookkeeper (frontend-only Next.js 16 / React 19 dashboard, mock data). Source is clean: 0 hardcoded secrets, 0 injection/eval/dangerouslySetInnerHTML, 0 PII logging, no API routes, no process.env reads in src/. Credential hygiene is correct (.env/.gcp/*.pem all gitignored; only .env.example tracked). Sole standing finding: postcss <8.5.10 XSS (GHSA, moderate) pulled in transitively via next@16.2.6 — ranked High per 'known-CVE' policy but low real reachability (build-time CSS only). Remediation is a package.json overrides pin postcss>=8.5.10, NOT the npm-suggested next@9.3.3 downgrade. Re-scans should check whether this override has landed (fixed) before re-reporting. Watch L1: secrets (GEMINI/PLAID/TWILIO/STRIPE) are declared in .env.example but unused — when server code is added, ensure no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix on secret keys.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 18:53 UTC
ai-bookkeeper versioning: pre-1.0 minor-for-features; v0.3.0 ledger money layer
content: ai-bookkeeper follows a pre-1.0 (0.x) semver policy where BREAKING changes increment the MINOR (documented in CHANGELOG header). v0.2.0 was the platform-migration release (Next 14→16, React 18→19, Tailwind 3→4, TS 5→6) — breaking but minor under that policy. v0.3.0 (2026-05-27) is the ledger money layer: currency-aware banker's rounding (roundMoney/minorUnits/MINOR_UNITS, ADR 0002, round-half-to-even), input-boundary amount validation, and the transaction ingest seam — all backward-compatible features → MINOR. Recurring release-integrity hazard: src/features/ledger/index.ts barrel re-exports lib modules (amount.ts, ingest.ts) that are frequently UNTRACKED in git while round.ts is committed — verify all barrel-referenced files are committed before cutting a tag. postcss is pinned ^8.5.15 directly, so the transitive XSS advisory is already remediated.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 18:57 UTC
ai-bookkeeper v0.3.0 docs already in sync at release
content: When the v0.3.0 release.published event fired, the README, CHANGELOG [0.3.0] section, and docs/releases/v0.3.0.md already documented all three ledger features (banker's rounding, amount validation, ingest boundary) accurately — no doc edits needed. Currency-verification no-op. Last-processed commit watermark set to b29eeaa. Lesson: the Release Manager's pipeline appears to author docs before publishing the release event, so Docs Steward runs triggered by release.published will frequently be confirmation/no-op runs rather than edit runs — that is still value-delivering currency verification.
